titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Juana de Laban Papers LSC.1422
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Yasmin Damshenas with assistance from Megan Fraser in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), 2011 July; machine-readable finding aid created by Caroline Cubé. Finding aid for accession LSC-2264-2014-11 prepared by Sadie Baldwin with assistance from Emily Wittenberg in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), 2026 May.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 May 20 by Sadie Baldwin.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-29 11:29:09 -0700.

did.unittitle: Juana de Laban papers

physdesc.extent: (6 slide boxes, 4 boxes, 1 half box, 1 flat box, 1 shoebox)

did.abstract: Juana de Laban (1910-1978) was a dancer, teacher, choreographer, critic, and scholar who served as the director of graduate studies in theater at Baylor University, was a distinguished professor at the University of Wisconsin, and was a dance faculty member at UCLA for nearly a decade. The collection consists of publications, periodicals, performance programs, photographs, writings, research notes, dance notation materials, and a slide collection with related card catalogs and instructional files. The materials document de Laban's interests in dance history, notation, Hungarian dance, modern dance, ballet, Denishawn, Japanese dance, and visual resources for the study and teaching of dance.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.
did.langmaterial: Materials are in English, Hungarian, German, Japanese, and French.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Juana de Laban papers (Collection 1422). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Yasmin Damshenas with assistance from Megan Fraser in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), July 2011.

Reprocessed by Sadie Baldwin with assistance from Emily Wittenberg in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), incorporating the accession LSC-2264-2014-11 and substantially revising the arrangement and container list, May 2026.
The collection was reprocessed to replace a previous inventory-style arrangement with a series-based arrangement reflecting the functions and formats of the materials.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Juana de Laban was a notable figure in the dance world, having participated in the field as a dancer, teacher, choreographer, director, critic and scholar. Of Hungarian descent, Juana de Laban von Varalja was born on October 21, 1910 en route from Austria to Switzerland. She was the daughter of Rudolf von Laban, the inventor of the system of Labanotation, a groundbreaking method of notating and interpreting human movement. De Laban studied with the Institute of Choreographics in Berlin and the Royal Hungarian Academy in Budapest. She spent time working for European film companies TERRA and URA and became known for her interpretation of native Hungarian dances. With the start of World War II, de Laban relocated to Texas and eventually received her master's and doctoral degrees in dramatic theory and criticism from Yale University. She is said to be the recipient of the first PhD ever granted in the U.S. for the study of dance criticism. De Laban became director of graduate studies in theater at Baylor University. She went on to serve on the dance faculty at UCLA for eight years, during which time she started a free lecture series, worked to build the library in the area of dance history and aesthetics, and helped Library Special Collections acquire the Ruth St. Denis papers. She was active in many educational and professional organizations, including the Committee on Research in Dance, Sacred Dance Guild of America and the Dallas Theater Center. In the fall of 1971, de Laban moved back to Texas as director of the graduate program in dance at Southern Methodist University. In 1973 she relocated to Temple, Texas where she later died on July 4, 1978. Her publications include Dance Notation (c. 1946).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains Juana de Laban's research, teaching, and collecting materials related to dance history, notation, performance, and criticism. Materials include correspondence, typed drafts and carbon copies, notes, sketches, clippings, photographs, books, pamphlets, dance programs, magazines, journals, and slides. The slide materials consist chiefly of a dance history slide collection arranged according to an alphabetical subject classification system with accompanying card catalog and master cards. Languages represented include English, Hungarian, German, Japanese, and French.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p:
The collection is arranged in four series and sub-series:
Series 1: Publications and printed material
Subseries 1.1: Dance periodicals and serial publications
Subseries 1.2: Books and pamphlets on dance history and notation
Subseries 1.3: Dance programs and souvenir programs
Subseries 1.4: Ruth St. Denis, Ted Shawn, and Denishawn printed material
Subseries 1.5: Hungarian dance and cultural publications
Subseries 1.6: Japanese dance, theater, and cultural publications
Series 2: Dance history slides and related cataloging files
Sub-series 2.1: Slide card catalog and related files
Sub-series 2.2: Dance history slide sequence
Sub-series 2.3: Supplemental and unfiled slides
Series 3: Photographs
Series 4: Writings and research materials
This arrangement was imposed during reprocessing.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Elyse Robert collection on Ruth St. Denis, 1932-1991 (Collection 1735). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.
Margerie Lyon Collection about Ted Shawn and the Dance (Collection 1478). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

Reflections on a life in the dance. Ruth St. Denis, interviewee. UCLA Oral History Department interview, 1965. UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.
Ruth St. Denis Papers (Collection 1031). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.
Ted Shawn correspondence with John Dougherty (Collection 1859). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

did.unittitle: Series 1: Publications and printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of books, periodicals, serials, dance programs, souvenir programs, and related printed materials collected by Juana de Laban. The materials document dance history, notation, aesthetics, ballet, modern dance, Denishawn, Hungarian dance, Japanese dance, and performance culture. The series includes publications by or about Juana de Laban, Rudolf von Laban, Ruth St. Denis, Ted Shawn, Isadora Duncan, Martha Graham, Anna Pavlova, and other figures in dance history.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into six sub-series by format and subject: Sub-series 1.1: Dance periodicals and serial publications Sub-series 1.2: Books and pamphlets on dance history and notation Sub-series 1.3: Dance programs and souvenir programs Sub-series 1.4: Ruth St. Denis, Ted Shawn, and Denishawn printed material Sub-series 1.5: Hungarian dance and cultural publications Sub-series 1.6: Japanese dance, theater, and cultural publications

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 1.1: Dance periodicals and serial publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains dance periodicals and serial publications dating from 1946 to 1975. Included are issues of Dance Index, Dance Magazine, and Dance Perspectives. The publications address dance notation, dance aesthetics, dance history, modern dance, ballet, Ghanian dance, dance under National Socialism, ballet de cour, and the relationship between dance and poetry. The Dance Perspectives issues include works by or edited by Jack Anderson, Lincoln Kirstein, George Beiswanger, Odette Blum, Horst Koegler, Erik Aschengreen, and John H. Baron. These publications provide critical, historical, and scholarly perspectives on twentieth-century dance and earlier dance traditions.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged by publication title and issue chronology. Individual issues of Dance Perspectives are arranged numerically and chronologically where possible.

did.unittitle: de Laban, Juana. "Dance Notation." Dance Index 5, no. 4-5. New York: Dance Index-Ballet Caravan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Has a sheet of notes inserted. This issue also includes an article by Juana de Laban's father, Rudolf von Laban.

did.unittitle: Dance Magazine 38, no.8
Dance Perspectives, nos. 52, 54-58, 61-62

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: Anderson, Jack, ed. "Dance, the Dancer, and the Poem: An Anthology of Twentieth Century Poems." Dance Perspectives, no. 52, Winter 1972. Kirstein, Lincoln. "Entries from an Early Diary." Dance Perspectives, no. 54, Summer 1973. Beiswanger, George. "Three Essays in Dance Aesthetics." Dance Perspectives, no. 55, Autumn 1973. Blum, Odette. "Dance in Ghana." Dance Perspectives, no. 56, Winter 1973. Koegler, Horst. "In the Shadow of the Swastika." Dance Perspectives, no. 57, Spring 1974. Aschengreen, Erik. "Beautiful Danger." Dance Perspectives, no. 58, Summer 1974. "All that strange & mysterious folk." Dance Perspectives 16, no. 1 / no. 61, Spring 1975. Baron, John H. "Les Fées des Forests de S. Germain: Ballet de Cour, 1625." Dance Perspectives 16, no. 2 / no. 62, 1975.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 1.2: Books and pamphlets on dance history and notation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains books and pamphlets on dance history, notation, modern dance, photographic documentation of dance, and related artistic traditions. Materials date from 1830 to 1970 and undated. Included are works by or about Carlo Blasis, Ernst Blass, Isadora Duncan, Rudolf Laban, Barbara Morgan, V.I. Stepanov, Anna Pavlova, and Marian Van Tuyl.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged alphabetically by author or title, with undated materials retained within the topical or alphabetical sequence.

did.unittitle: Blasis, M. Manuel complet de la danse. France: Librairie Encyclopédique
Blass, Ernst. Das Wesen der neuen Tanzkunst. Weimar: Erich Lichtenstein Verlag
Books & Scores from the Bureau. New York: Dance Notation Bureau
Dancers of Bali. New York: Lou Dunetz and Paul Lovett Publishers
Die Elizabeth Duncan-Schule. Jena, Germany: Eugen Diederichs
Duncan, Irma. Isadora Duncan: Pioneer in the Art of Dance. New York: New York Public Library
Gauguin, Paul. Noa Noa. Berlin: Verlag von Bruno Cassirer
Laban, Rudolf. Choreutics. London: Macdonald and Evans
Morgan, Barbara. Martha Graham: Sixteen Dances in Photographs. New York: Duell, Sloan & Pearce
Stepanov, V.I. Alphabet of Movements. Cambridge: Golden Head Press

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Number 6 of 175 copies printed for sale in 1958.

did.unittitle: Tänze von Anna Pawlowa im Bilde. Dresden: Carl Reissner Verlag

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes press clippings inserted in between book pages.

did.unittitle: Van Tuyl, Marian. Extensions of Dance. San Francisco: Impulse Publications
Sub-Series 1.3: Dance programs and souvenir programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains dance programs and souvenir programs for ballet and modern dance companies, dating from 1957 to 1974 and undated. Included are programs for London's Festival Ballet, the Martha Graham Dance Company, New York City Ballet, and Roland Petit's Ballets de Paris. The programs document repertory, performers, companies, and performance presentation practices. They also provide visual and textual evidence of mid-twentieth-century dance promotion, company identity, and audience engagement.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged alphabetically by company or program title.

did.unittitle: Williams, Peter ed. London's Festival Ballet. New York: Artcraft Litho and Printing Co., Inc.
Leabo, Karl ed. Martha Graham Dance Company. New York: The Martha Graham Center for Contemporary Dance
New York City Ballet Programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: New York City Ballet Program. New York: New York Center of Music & Drama, 1957. New York City Ballet Program. Place of publication not identified: publisher not identified, undated.

did.unittitle: Roland Petit's Ballets de Paris in Carmen and Other Ballets. New York: Benj. Weitzner
Sub-Series 1.4: Ruth St. Denis, Ted Shawn, and Denishawn printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains printed material related to Ruth St. Denis, Ted Shawn, and the Denishawn School of Dancing. Materials date from 1920 to 1967 and undated. It documents Denishawn's role in American modern dance, dance pedagogy, performance culture, and the careers of Ruth St. Denis and Ted Shawn. The materials also reflect the school's self-presentation and its influence on modern dance history.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged by publication type and subject.

did.unittitle: Shawn, Ted. Ruth St. Denis: Pioneer & Prophet, volumes I and II. San Francisco: John Henry Nash

odd.head: General note
odd.p: This is the printer's copy, only 350 copies were printed.

did.unittitle: The Denishawn Magazine: A Quarterly Review Devoted to the Art of Dance. New York: The Ruth St. Denis and Ted Shawn School of Dancing, vol. 1, no. 1

The Denishawn Magazine: A Quarterly Review Devoted to the Art of Dance. New York: The Ruth St. Denis and Ted Shawn School of Dancing, vol. 1, no. 4
First Lady of the Dance. Los Angeles: Burns
Schlundt, Christena, L. Ruth St. Denis & Ted Shawn. New York: New York Public Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains letter from Schlundt to de Laban, dated October 18, 1963.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 1.5: Hungarian dance and cultural publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains Hungarian dance and cultural publications dating from 1952 to 1968 and undated. The materials document Hungarian folk dance, dance notation, dance scholarship, dance organizations, and broader Hungarian cultural production.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged primarily chronologically, with undated cultural publications placed at the end.

did.unittitle: Szentpál, Mária ed. A Kultúrverseny Legszebb Táncai és Dalai. Budapest: Zenemukiadó Vállalat
Szentpál, Mária ed. Pusztafalutól Karcsáig Abaújmegyei Táncok és Dalok. Budapest: Zenemukiadó Vállalat
Martin, György. A Sárközi-Dunamenti Táncok Motívumkincse. Budapest: Népművelési Intézet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Detailed table of contents typed up in German inserted between pages 46-47.

did.unittitle: Martin, György. Sárközi-Dunamenti Motívumtár Melléklet. Budapest: Népművelési Intézet
Kaposi, Edit ed. Táncmuvészeti Értesíto. Budapest: A Magyar Táncmuvészek SzÖvetségének, vol. 1
Kaposi, Edit ed. Táncmuvészeti Értesíto. Budapest: A Magyar Táncmuvészek SzÖvetségének, vol. 2
Kaposi, Edit ed. Táncmuvészeti Értesíto. Budapest: A Magyar Táncmuvészek SzÖvetségének, vol. 1
Kaposi, Edit ed. A Magyar Táncmuvészet Hete: Szakmai Vitáirol Készült JegyzokÖnyv. Budapest: publisher not identified
Arany, János. Összes kÖltoi muvei. Budapest: Franklin-Tarsulat
Oláh, Gustáv. and Ferenc Szántó, eds. Magyar Balletek. Budapest: Az Officina Nyomda

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Book on Hungarian ballet written in Hungarian, German, French, Italian and English.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 1.6: Japanese dance, theater, and cultural publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains Japanese dance, theater, and cultural publications dating from 1926 to 1954 and undated. It documents Japanese dance and theatrical traditions, including studies of movement, performance, and dramatic forms.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged primarily chronologically, with undated publications placed at the end.

did.unittitle: Matsuoka, Eikyu. Zuroku emakimono shoshaku. Tokyo: Morie Shoten
Kuni, Masami. Buyo gaisetsu. Tokyo: Kuni Buyo Kenkyujo

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Book is a gift from Masami Kuni.

did.unittitle: Kuni, Masami. Buyo. Tokyo: Taiiku no Kagakusha

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Black and white photo of dance school instructors inserted in book; book is a gift from the author, Masami Kuni.

did.unittitle: Kuni, Masami. Ugoki no rizumu. Tokyo: Ban'yusha

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Book is a gift from Masami Kuni.

did.unittitle: Florenz, Karl. Japanische Dramen: Terakoya und Asagao. Leipzig: C.F. Amelangs Verlag
Series 2: Dance history slides and related cataloging files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains a dance history slide collection and related cataloging files. The materials include slide card catalogs, master cards, lists, instructions, source-book notes, and a structured slide sequence organized by historical period, geographic region, dance form, performer, company, and theme. The series also includes supplemental and unfiled slides, such as ballet costume and fashion slides, museum and gallery slides, duplicate slides, and unlabeled metal-framed slides.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into three sub-series: Sub-series 2.1: Slide card catalog and related files Sub-series 2.2: Dance history slide sequence Sub-series 2.3: Supplemental and unfiled slides The main slide sequence is arranged according to the alphabetical section system, A through T, reflecting historical, geographic, biographical, and thematic categories.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 2.1: Slide card catalog and related files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains cataloging files related to the dance history slide collection. Materials include catalog instructions for use, a slide card catalog, lists of slides, reports on source books, master cards, cards identifying books from which no pictures were to be taken, and cards for slides to be made. The files document the organization, description, and creation of the slide collection. They provide evidence of the intellectual structure of the slide sequence and the process by which images were selected, cataloged, and prepared for instructional or research use.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged by function, beginning with instructions and catalogs, followed by lists, reports, master cards, exclusion cards, and production cards.

did.unittitle: Catalog instructions for use
Slides card catalog, Primitive A- Comedy Film Variety T-
List of slides
Dr. Laban: a report on the books
Set of master cards
Cards of books from which no pictures are to be taken
Cards to make slides of
Sub-Series 2.2: Dance history slide sequence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains the main dance history slide sequence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged according to the existing alphabetical section sequence, A through T. Sections are organized by historical period, geographic tradition, dance movement, performer or company, and thematic category. Sub-sections within Section O document German modern dance figures and movements.

Sub-Series 2.3: Supplemental and unfiled slides

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains supplemental and unfiled slides related to dance, costume, museums, galleries, and visual culture.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged by slide grouping or original label. Duplicate and unlabeled slides are filed at the end.

did.unittitle: Ballet costume and fashion slides, 19th century
Firenze, Galleria degli Uffizi slides
Mixed museum and gallery slides
Duplicate slides
Unlabeled metal-framed slides
Series 3: Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs of dancers and dance-related subjects.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged by subject or photographer.

did.unittitle: Juana de Laban at UCLA
Juana de Laban at the UCLA Artist Series
Photographs by John Snyder of dancers
Ruth St. Denis
Series 4: Writings and research materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains draft writings and research notes related to "Magyar Dances." The series closely relates to the Hungarian dance publications in sub-series 1.5.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series consists of one grouping of draft and research materials related to "Magyar Dances."

did.unittitle: Magyar Dances, draft and research notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: In English, Hungarian and German, includes sketch, dance notations, glossary, discography for records, correspondence, press clippings, musical scores, flyers, book titled Magyar Táncok from ca. 1933.
